title: Share of people who have depressive disorders, by age
subtitle: Estimated share of people who have  [depressive disorders](#dod:depressive_disorders), whether or not they are diagnosed, based on representative surveys, medical data and statistical modelling.
caption:
Share of people who have depressive disorders, by age

Estimated share of people who have  depressive disorders, whether or not they are diagnosed, based on representative surveys, medical data and statistical modelling.

This horizontal bar chart displays the estimated share of people with depressive disorders across various age groups for the world in 2023. The data indicates that the prevalence of these disorders is lowest among children aged 5 to 14, at 0.98%. The share rises significantly for the 15 to 19 age group to 4.55% and continues to increase through young adulthood, reaching 5% for those aged 30 to 34. The highest prevalence rates are observed in older age demographics. The share peaks at 5.46% for individuals aged 65 to 69, with the adjacent groups of ages 60 to 64 and ages 70 plus recording values of 5.4% and 5.36% respectively. The middle-aged cohorts from 35 to 64 maintain relatively stable shares, clustering tightly between 5.14% and 5.4%. In contrast to the specific age breakdowns, the age-standardized share is recorded at 3.81%, while the aggregate figure for all ages combined stands at 4%. The visual arrangement highlights a distinct trend where the estimated share of people with depressive disorders is substantially higher in adult and elderly populations compared to children and adolescents.
